name: CatVTON
description: [ICLR 2025] CatVTON is a simple and efficient virtual try-on diffusion model with 1) Lightweight Network (899.06M parameters totally), 2) Parameter-Efficient Training (49.57M parameters trainable) and 3) Simplified Inference (< 8G VRAM for 1024X768 resolution).
readme_summary: CatVTON is an efficient diffusion model for virtual clothing try-on. It features a lightweight network architecture, parameter-efficient training, and simplified inference, allowing high-resolution try-ons with minimal VRAM.
cover_image_prompt: A fashion designer stands in a magical workshop, holding a plain mannequin. With a wave of their hand, a digital projection of a person appears, and a chosen garment from a rack of vibrant clothes seamlessly drapes onto the projected figure, perfectly fitting its form. The input is a photograph of a person and a separate image of clothing; the output is the person virtually wearing the clothing. Subtle UI elements showing clothing selection and pose adjustments are visible on a nearby holographic screen. The scene is bathed in soft, creative light, with glowing data particles flowing from the designer's hand to the projection. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a modern, elegant aesthetic, emphasizing the transformation and ease of use.

name: tickrs
description: Realtime ticker data in your terminal 📈
readme_summary: Tickrs is a Rust-based command-line application that provides real-time stock ticker data directly in the terminal. It leverages Yahoo! Finance for data and offers various customization options for displaying stock information, charts, and market hours.
key_features: Configurable time frames and update intervals
Multiple installation methods (binary, cargo, package managers)
primary_use_case: Displaying real-time stock market data and charts in a terminal environment.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, glowing terminal window floating in a digital space, displaying vibrant, real-time stock charts and ticker symbols. Data streams, visualized as glowing particles, flow from a subtle Yahoo! Finance logo towards the terminal. A small, stylized graph icon is integrated into the terminal's interface, representing the charting capabilities. The overall mood is dynamic and informative, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The art style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a dark background, emphasizing the glowing elements and data flow.

name: seeker
description: Accurately Locate Smartphones using Social Engineering
readme_summary: Seeker is a tool designed to accurately locate smartphones by hosting a fake website that requests location permissions. Upon user consent, it gathers precise location data (longitude, latitude, accuracy, altitude) and device information (unique ID, model, OS, hardware specs, IP addresses) through social engineering tactics. It serves as a proof-of-concept to educate users about potential data privacy risks from malicious websites.
key_features: Hosts fake websites to request location permissions
Gathers Longitude, Latitude, Accuracy, Altitude, Direction, and Speed
Collects Device Information via Canvas Fingerprinting (Unique ID, Model, OS, CPU Cores, RAM, Screen Resolution, GPU, Browser, Public/Local IP)
Provides higher accuracy than traditional IP Geolocation by using GPS hardware
Includes various pre-made templates (NearYou, Google Drive, WhatsApp, etc.)
primary_use_case: To demonstrate and educate users about how malicious websites can exploit location permissions and gather sensitive device information through social engineering, acting as a proof-of-concept for security awareness.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, slightly mischievous-looking smartphone character holding a glowing compass, with its location pinpointed on a stylized map that is subtly integrated into a web browser window. Faint digital particles representing collected data (like longitude, latitude, and device specs) float around the phone and map. In the background, a subtle, abstract representation of a 'fake' website interface is visible, hinting at the social engineering aspect. The overall mood is informative and cautionary, using a clean, modern illustration style with a focus on clarity and a color palette of blues, greens, and a touch of warning red for the data points. The repository name 'Seeker' is subtly integrated into the browser's URL bar.

name: NeoShell
description: Управляй своим ПК с телефона через Wi-Fi.
readme_summary: NeoShell is a Python-based application that allows users to remotely control their Windows PC from a smartphone via Wi-Fi. It provides a web-based interface accessible from any device on the same network, offering features like PC locking, sleep, shutdown, task management, and application launching.
key_features: Progressive Web App (PWA) support for mobile
primary_use_case: Remotely managing and controlling a Windows PC from a smartphone or other web-enabled device over a local Wi-Fi network.
cover_image_prompt: A person relaxing on a couch, holding a smartphone that displays a sleek, futuristic dashboard. From the smartphone, a glowing, ethereal beam of light connects to a stylized, simplified representation of a desktop computer. The computer is shown performing actions like dimming its screen (sleep) or having its windows neatly stacked and minimized. Subtle digital particles flow from the phone to the PC, indicating control. The background is a comfortable, modern living room. The overall mood is one of effortless convenience and power. The art style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, vibrant color palette, emphasizing the seamless connection between the phone and the PC.

name: Gource
readme_summary: Gource is a C++ visualization tool that renders source control repository history as an interactive 3D tree. It visually represents directories as branches and files as leaves, with contributors appearing and disappearing as they make changes. The tool allows for real-time playback and customization of the visualization.
key_features: Tree-based display of directories and files
primary_use_case: Visualizing and understanding the history and evolution of source code repositories in an engaging and interactive 3D environment.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, stylized 3D tree growing dynamically in a digital space. Each branch represents a directory, and leaves represent files. Tiny, diverse avatars of developers are seen actively tending to specific branches and leaves, making them glow or change color as they commit changes. Small, subtle UI elements showing commit messages and dates float around the tree. The repository name 'Gource' is subtly integrated into the roots of the tree. The overall mood is one of collaborative creation and growth. The art style should be a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration with a bright, engaging color palette.
